title: cieca litoralis
intro: Cieca litoralis, also known as sea lavender or rosemary of the sea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cieca litoralis include reducing stress and anxiety, improving cognitive function, and providing relief from digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Cieca litoralis can be found in teas, infusions, and topical creams, and it is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and improve mood.
